prompt: Branch bugfix/MED-842-partial-read on a repo whose history uses ticket-prefixed subjects (`MED-XXX: Subject`). Working tree: reader.rs bug fix + tests/reader.rs + unrelated serde bump. Commit them.

assertions.text: Produced exactly two new commits beyond the three original ticket-prefixed commits
Bug-fix commit groups src/reader.rs + tests/reader.rs together (one commit, not two)
Dep-bump commit (Cargo.toml) is separate from the bug-fix commit
Bug-fix commit subject uses ticket-prefixed style matching history (e.g. 'MED-842: ...')
Bug-fix commit explicitly references MED-842 (either in subject prefix or as a Refs:/Closes:/Fixes: footer)
Bug-fix commit body explains before/after behavior (truncated reads → full accumulation, or equivalent)
Dep-bump commit subject is reasonable (does NOT reference MED-842, since the bump is unrelated)
No commit message contains tooling attribution (no 'Claude', no 'AI', no 'verified by')
